suggested.why: 3 Acme/Zenith deals had no reply in 7+ days
Jira shows 4 status changes since last sync

suggested.text: Assign objection-handling Box to J. Doe
suggested.why: Lost-deal analysis flags objection handling as the gap

pulse.overnight: Brain summarized 4 threads · found 1 new gap · ran 2 flows.

cards.title: Email to J. (Acme)
cards.preview: Hi James, following up on the renewal — happy to walk through the new tiers…

boxes.name: Negotiation Arena
boxes.assignedNote: Prep for Acme pitch next week

boxes.name: Code Combat
boxes.assignedNote: Code review skills for Q3 sprint

box-negotiation.name: Negotiation Arena

turns.text: Your competitor offered 30% less. Match it.

box-negotiation.reply: I hear you, but my budget is firm — give me a reason to pay more.
box-negotiation.coach: Anchor on value first, then trade — never give a discount for free.

box-objection.name: Objection Handling

turns.text: Honestly, this costs more than we budgeted.

box-objection.reply: Okay… but I still need to justify this to my boss.
box-objection.coach: Acknowledge, then reframe around ROI and cost of inaction.

box-code.name: Code Combat
box-code.scene: A function returns the wrong total for empty carts. Find and fix the bug.

turns.text: Tests are red on the empty-cart case. What's your first move?

box-code.reply: Good instinct — walk me through why that branch fails.
box-code.coach: Reproduce with the smallest failing input before changing code.

box-crisis.name: Crisis Room
box-crisis.scene: Prod is down, 3 Slack pings and a customer call arrive at once. Prioritize.

turns.text: Sev-1: checkout is failing. Where do you start?

box-crisis.reply: Got it — who do you pull in and what do you tell customers?
box-crisis.coach: Stabilize first, communicate second, root-cause third.

box-design.name: Design Studio
box-design.scene: Users drop off at onboarding step 2. Redesign the flow.

turns.text: Here's the research brief. What persona do you design for first?

box-design.reply: Interesting — why that persona over the power user?
box-design.coach: Start from the job-to-be-done, not the screen.

box-pyoop.name: Python OOP Sandbox
box-pyoop.scene: Implement a BankAccount class with deposit/withdraw and overdraft protection.

turns.text: Sandbox ready. Define your class, then run.

box-pyoop.reply: ✓ ran — 2/3 tests pass · overdraft case still throws.
box-pyoop.coach: Start with __init__, validate the amount type, then guard the balance.

box-k8s.name: Kubernetes Sandbox
box-k8s.scene: Deploy an app, scale it to 3 replicas, then debug a CrashLoopBackOff.
box-k8s.reply: $ pods: 2/3 Running, 1 CrashLoopBackOff — check the logs.
box-k8s.coach: Describe the pod and read the last logs before editing the manifest.

box-rabbit.name: RabbitMQ Sandbox
box-rabbit.scene: Build a producer/consumer and route failures to a dead-letter queue.
box-rabbit.reply: ✓ messages flowing — 4 failed and landed in the DLQ.
box-rabbit.coach: Set the DLX on the main queue before publishing.

box-sql.name: SQL Tuning Lab
box-sql.scene: A report query takes 9s. Profile it and add the right index.

turns.text: EXPLAIN shows a full table scan on orders.

box-sql.coach: Index the columns in the WHERE + JOIN, watch selectivity.

search.query: what did we decide about Acme pricing?

commands.text: Create a flow for standup collection

acme-f.name: Acme Corp
acme-f.workflowText: When a new lead email arrives in Gmail, enrich it with company data, draft a personalized outreach in our brand voice, and stage it for my approval. Every Monday 9am compile a pipeline report and store it in the Renewal reports sub-folder.

nodes.name: Ingest leads
nodes.prompt: Extract new B2B leads from inbox; skip newsletters and no-reply senders.

nodes.name: Enrich firmographics
nodes.prompt: Add company size, funding stage, tech stack for each lead.

nodes.name: Enrich intent signals
nodes.prompt: Pull recent intent signals (site visits, hiring, news) for each lead.

nodes.name: Draft outreach
nodes.prompt: Write a warm, concise personalized DM per lead. Lead with value, not discount.

nodes.name: Stage for approval
nodes.prompt: Push each draft to the Action Queue as a Draft-tier card with evidence.

outputTo.note: Monday pipeline report is stored here → triggers the Renewal-reports workflow on arrival

minions.name: @Scout
minions.rules: B2B only · ignore newsletters · dedupe by domain

minions.name: @Mary
minions.rules: Brand voice: warm, concise · value before price

acme-f.outputStyle: Concise bullets + a one-line TL;DR at top. Tables for numbers, never walls of text.
acme-f.teamwork: Scout hands enriched leads to Mary via leads.json; Mary asks Scout to re-pull if a record is incomplete (loop on n1).

nodes.name: Escalate if risky

outputTo.note: Terminal — writes back to the parent board.

acme-reports-f.outputStyle: Risk-first: lead with the number and the recommended action.

acme-drafts-f.name: Outreach drafts
acme-drafts-f.workflowText: When drafts arrive from the parent folder, score each for reply-likelihood, tag the weakest, and keep an A/B variant.

nodes.name: Score drafts
nodes.prompt: Rate each draft 1-5 on clarity + reply-likelihood.

nodes.name: Make A/B variant
nodes.prompt: For low-scoring drafts, generate a punchier variant.

outputTo.note: Terminal — writes the scored set back to the parent folder.

acme-drafts-f.outputStyle: Score table + one A/B variant per weak draft.
acme-drafts-f.teamwork: Receives drafts from Acme Corp; returns the scored set to the parent board.

draft.name: renewal_memo.md
draft.md:
# Acme Corp — Renewal Memo

**Prepared by** @Mary · draft v0.3

## 1. Value delivered (Q2)
- 1,204 active workflow runs, up after the May dip
- 18 support tickets resolved, CSAT 94%
- 2 expansion use-cases adopted

## 2. ROI
- Est. **$210k** analyst-hours saved this quarter
- Payback on current spend: **~3.1 months**

## 3. Recommended terms
- **6% uplift** vs list
- **2-year lock** to de-risk churn
- Quarterly business review included

## 4. Risks
- Champion is new in seat — keep exec sponsor close
- Competitor undercutting on price (see positioning_map)

conversation.text: On pricing I'd propose 6% uplift + 2-yr lock to de-risk churn — within policy?
Approved. @Mary finalise with that, @Frank route to legal once it's ready.

Indexed 6 competitors + 320 reviews. competitor_signals.json is up.
Whitespace found on 'time-to-value' — we under-claim it. Drafting messaging next.

draft.name: positioning_brief.md
draft.md:
# Positioning Gap — Time-to-Value

## The gap
Competitors lead with **speed to first result**; we bury it.

## Evidence
- 4/6 competitors put 'live in minutes' above the fold
- 38% of our reviews praise speed — we don't claim it

## Recommended messaging
- **Hero:** "From data to decision in one afternoon."
- Add a time-to-value proof point on pricing page
- Re-cut the demo to show first result < 5 min

conversation.text: Great find. Prioritise that gap for the messaging recommendations.

queue.back: The value you deliver — never lead with a discount.

tutor.greeting: Hi! I'm your AI tutor. Tell me what to get better at — I'll teach the gap, not the whole topic.
suggestions: Why did my Crisis Room score drop?

gapAlerts.title: Negotiation Anchoring
gapAlerts.reason: Your Acme deal closed 23% below target — Doty analysis detected no anchor was established before the buyer countered.
examples: Acme Q2 deal: accepted buyer's first counter (€82k) without setting an opening position — left ~€22k on the table
Zenith pitch: price discussion started from their number, not yours, giving them the anchor advantage throughout

roadmap.name: Study BTNA anchor theory
roadmap.desc: Understand the psychology of anchoring: why the first number sets the negotiation range and how to use it both offensively and defensively.

roadmap.name: Anchor drill in Negotiation Arena
roadmap.desc: Run 3 guided sessions in the Negotiation Arena box. Goal: open every sim with a bold anchor before the buyer speaks.

roadmap.name: Mock deal with Omar
roadmap.desc: Schedule a 30-min mock negotiation — Omar plays buyer, you anchor first and hold for 2 rounds.

roadmap.name: Apply in next real deal
roadmap.desc: Use the anchor technique in the next client call. Log the opening position and outcome in your deal notes.

gapAlerts.title: Async Error Handling Patterns
gapAlerts.reason: 3 recent code reviews flagged unhandled promise rejections and missing try/catch in async flows.
examples: Memory pipeline: vector upsert in analysis ETL has no error boundary — a Pinecone timeout silently drops documents
API routes: 2 endpoints have unhandled rejections that surface as 500s with no retry or fallback

roadmap.name: Review the 5 async error patterns
roadmap.desc: Study: try/catch, .catch(), error boundaries, global unhandledRejection, and retry-with-backoff. Understand when to use each.

roadmap.name: Fix identified issues in codebase
roadmap.desc: Add error handling to the 3 flagged points in the memory pipeline and API routes. Add structured logging per failure.

roadmap.name: Code Combat sandbox practice
roadmap.desc: Run the Code Combat sandbox with async debugging scenarios to build muscle memory for error wrapping.

gapAlerts.title: System Design Trade-off Analysis
gapAlerts.reason: Tutor sessions show strong breadth but shallow depth on consistency model trade-offs — CAP theorem scenarios repeated across 3 sessions without resolution.
examples: Tutor Q&A: explained CAP theorem correctly but couldn't articulate partition-tolerance trade-offs in a real distributed counter scenario
Architecture discussion: defaulted to strong consistency without evaluating the latency cost or eventual-consistency alternatives

roadmap.name: Deep-read consistency models
roadmap.desc: Study eventual consistency, CRDTs, and quorum reads. Focus on real examples: Cassandra, DynamoDB, Spanner. Not theory — how each trade-off manifests in prod.

roadmap.name: Design a distributed counter from scratch
roadmap.desc: Design a distributed counter with 3 different consistency requirements. Write a one-page trade-off analysis for each.

roadmap.name: Audit Doty's memory pipeline
roadmap.desc: Apply the framework to Doty's own architecture. Document where consistency vs availability trade-offs were made and whether they were the right calls.

longTermGoals.title: Become a confident B2B enterprise closer
longTermGoals.desc: Own the full enterprise sales cycle — from qualification to close — without relying on external advisors.

longTermGoals.title: Production-grade distributed systems depth
longTermGoals.desc: Move from breadth to depth — be able to design, defend, and debug distributed systems under constraint.

verifyRequests.note: Preparing for the Zenith enterprise deal — need to confirm negotiation readiness before the Q3 pitch.
Infra sprint starts next week — confirm distributed systems depth before the architecture review.

channels.name: dev
channels.desc: Engineering, code reviews and architecture decisions

channels.name: ai-research
channels.desc: LLM experiments, agent logs and research links

ch-general.text: Agenda: Zenith deal update -> pipeline review -> Q3 targets. Should be 20 mins max.
Ready. I have an update on the BTNA session too.
Notes from the stand-up are in Notion. Osama branding assets are in the shared folder!
Zenith confirmed Thursday 14:00 for the close call. Lets be sharp 🎯
Anchoring at 120k, floor 95k. Will run through the BTNA deck tonight.

ch-dev.text: PR #47 is up memory pipeline idempotency fix. Anyone free for a review?
Looking at it now. Idempotency logic looks solid. Error handling in the retry path needs a DLQ hook though.
Good catch. Left a comment on line 47. Adding the DLQ in next commit.
Left two more comments. Overall LGTM — merge-ready after those fixes. 🟢
Should we add a circuit breaker to the vector insert step? Seeing timeouts under load.
Yes. Exponential backoff + max 3 retries then dead-letter queue. Adding a sprint card.

ch-product.text: Updated the Zenith use-case doc. New section on async API pain points.
Q3 product roadmap ready for review: 4 features, 2 infra items, 1 compliance item.
The AI-native diff viewer is highest leverage — we should front-load it in Q3.
Onboarding flow needs a pass — drop-off at step 3 per latest cohort data.
Scheduling a UX audit session next week. Setting up the Figma board now.

ch-sales.text: Zenith close call Thursday 14:00. High priority — evaluating two other vendors.
On it. Running BTNA prep tonight. Anchor 120k, floor 95k. Strategy doc by tomorrow AM.
Acme renewal in 3 weeks. Call with their ops lead on Friday.
Reference the Q2 reports data — they care about ROI metrics above everything.

ch-ai.text: Testing context caching on the Company Brain pipeline. 60% cost reduction on repeated chunks 🤯
That is huge. Stable enough for prod?
Still testing edge cases — cache invalidation on chunk updates is tricky. End of week.
Dropped a MIT paper in the folder — semantic graph embeddings for temporal context. Relevant to our memory pipeline.
Temporal decay weighting is exactly what we need for stale memory pruning 🔑
Should we add this to Q3 research backlog?
Yes — creating a learning box for it now. Summary by EOD.

dm-omar.text: Hey — did you see the BTNA Framework verification request? Can you complete it this week?
Yes, doing it tomorrow evening. The session design looks great.
The transcript review is the part I am most excited about — real signal on knowledge depth.
Also — Q3 investor deck needs your AI architecture section by EOD Friday. Around 4 slides.
On it. Covering retrieval pipeline, memory graph, agent orchestration, and cost benchmarks.

dm-sara.text: Sara, can you share the latest branding assets? Need them for the investor deck.
Sent! They are in the Doty Branding shared folder. Logo variants, colour tokens, and updated font stack.
Perfect, thank you! The new logo variant looks great.
I will also send the slide template with the new brand applied — should save time on the deck.

dm-flowna.text: Good morning! Quick summary of my learning progress this week?

Good morning, Osama! Here is your weekly snapshot:

• XP this month: 2,840 (up 12% vs last week)
• Active skills: BTNA Framework — verifying, System Design — learning
• SRS streak: 7 days — 4 cards due today
• Gap alerts: 3 pending review
• Verification from Omar: BTNA Framework — due this week 🎯
Create a learning box for the MIT temporal graph embeddings paper.

Done! Created Temporal Context in Semantic Graphs box under your AI Research track.

Pre-loaded 3 seed questions:
1. How does temporal decay weighting differ from static embeddings?
2. What are the trade-offs for stale memory pruning?
3. How would you apply this to the Doty retrieval pipeline?

teamGaps.title: System Design Under Constraints

teamGaps.title: Client-Facing Communication
teamGaps.reason: Engineers struggle to translate technical trade-offs into business language during client calls.
teamGaps.flownaStatus: Doty created a 5-step learning path: Active Listening -> Framing Trade-offs -> Simplifying Jargon -> Handling Pushback -> Practice Scenarios.

teamGaps.title: Async JavaScript and Error Handling
teamGaps.reason: Several production incidents traced back to unhandled promise rejections and missing retry logic.
teamGaps.suggestedPath: Async JS and Error Patterns (1 week)
